reason: Internal pathfinding tie-break fix: previously, two same-team units contesting a funnel corner whose uids happened to share parity (e.g. uid 0 and uid 2) got the identical routing lane offset and could still converge on the same waypoint. The fix widens the lane split from a 2-bucket uid-parity scheme to a fixed 3-bucket modulus (still a pure function of a unit's own uid, so a unit's lane never depends on which other units happen to be nearby), which resolves the reported collision for most uid pairs -- though two contesters exactly FUNNEL_LANE_COUNT apart can still collide, a residual gap tracked separately. The effect is a few world-units' difference in which lane a unit's routing corner lands on -- not reliably visually distinguishable in a recorded clip from ordinary crowd-physics jostling, and reproducing the exact same-parity-collision condition needs a contrived multi-unit setup rather than anything a normal battle demonstrates. Covered instead by GUT tests (test_funnel_lane_offset_separates_same_parity_uids, test_funnel_lane_offset_is_a_pure_function_of_uid_not_of_who_else_is_nearby) asserting on the actual lane values and the stability property.
